chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI C. P. No. S – 675 of 2025 Date Order with Signature of Judge 1.For order on CMA No.5363/25 (urgent) 2.For order on office objection No.1 to 4 & reply of counsel at Flag -A 3.For order on CMA No.5364/25 (exemption) 4.For hearing of main case 19.08.2025 Mr. Muhammad Ilyas Khan Tanoli, advocate for petitioner ---------- Petitioner, Mst. Qamar Nazir Ali, is a resident of Flat No.2C on the Second Floor of the Ali Sultan Building on Street No.3, Khayaban-e-Badar, Phase 5, Karachi. Respondent Nos. 5 to 10, are all private respondents, and carry on business on the Ground floor (allegedly running a restaurant), and/or reside on the Third and Fourth floors, as the case may be. Respondent No.11, also a private respondent, is the chowkidar in the said building. Respondent Nos.2 to 4 are officers of the Sindh Police. Respondent No.5 seeks/prays for favorable orders from this regular bench to restrain the respondents, their staff and everybody from advancing any threat to the petitioner and/or peaceful possession of the petitioner over her property. Additionally, Petitioner seeks to direct the Police Authorities to provide police protection, which is something that the police are required by law to do, as part of their public duty. Petitioner also prays that the Police Authorities take legal action against the private respondents for allegedly carrying on unlawful and immoral business in the building, including the basement. Earlier, Petitioner filed CP No.S-1242/2024 alleging nuisance on the part of the same private respondents and apprehending that they will dispossess her from her premises.

Petitioner also prays that the Police Authorities take legal action against the private respondents for allegedly carrying on unlawful and immoral business in the building, including the basement. Earlier, Petitioner filed CP No.S-1242/2024 alleging nuisance on the part of the same private respondents and apprehending that they will dispossess her from her premises. The said Petition was dismissed on 14.01.2025. No information is available on record that indicates fresh facts and/or circumstances since 14.01.2025 to date. Petitioner has an adequate alternative remedy available to her to pursue her grievance in both civil and criminal jurisdiction. She is at liberty to agitate the same in accordance with law. In the facts and circumstances, no case/relief is made out in writ jurisdiction. Accordingly, the petition is dismissed. J U D G E Ashraf
